title: sida guyanensis
intro: Sida guyanensis, also known as the Guyana sida or wild cotton, is a type of flowering plant in the Malvaceae family native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sida guyanensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Sida guyanensis include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be prepared by boiling the leaves and roots in water or using them as a paste to apply directly to affected areas.
